text:
We introduce a novel characterization of NP called Quadratic Span Programs (QSPs), a natural generalization of the span programs defined by Karchmer and Wigderson. Our primary contribution is a new approach to the arithmetization of Boolean circuits that bypasses the need for traditional Probabilistic Checkable Proofs (PCPs). By leveraging the algebraic properties of QSPs, we construct highly efficient succinct non-interactive zero-knowledge (NIZK) arguments.

In the common reference string (CRS) model, our QSP-based construction yields a NIZK for Circuit-SAT consisting of only 7 group elements, achieving the shortest proof size to date. Furthermore, our scheme features quasi-linear CRS size and prover computation, making it significantly more practical than prior techniques. We also introduce Quadratic Arithmetic Programs (QAPs), a variant of our characterization that natively handles arithmetic circuits over large fields. 

Finally, we demonstrate the utility of QSPs and QAPs in the context of publicly verifiable computation. Our resulting protocols allow a client to outsource the computation of a function $F(x)$ to an untrusted server and verify the returned result in substantially less time than a local execution would require. These constructions represent the most efficient general-purpose publicly verifiable computation schemes currently known.
